revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ayazuka Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Ayazuka Kofun | native_name =綾塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Ayazuka Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Ayazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ayazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|42|18|N|130|54|59|E|display=inline, title|qq=}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Miyako, Fukuoka|en|Miyako, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206868|lt=Miyako, Fukuoka|1=Miyako, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|42|18|N|130|54|59|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Ayazuka Kofun'''|綾塚古墳|qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Katsuyama Kuroda neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Miyako, Fukuoka|en|Miyako, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206868|lt=Miyako|1=Miyako, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Miyako District, Fukuoka|en|Miyako District, Fukuoka|qid=Q1156359|lt=Miyako District|1=Miyako District, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1973.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162428|title=綾塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Ayazuka Kofun is located on the hilly edge of the inland Miyako Plain facing the Gulf of Suo on the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=}}-style circular tumulus, with a diameter of 41 meters and height of eight meters, built in two stages. It has a surrounding moat of 4.5 meters in width. The internal structure is a multi-room horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] made of megaliths. The horizontal-passage burial chamber has a total length of 21 meters, which is the third largest in Japan after the [[Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|Mise Maruyama Kofun]] in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} and [[Miyajidake Kofun]] in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. The almost square rear chamber contains a house-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} 2.5 meters long, 1.6 meters wide, and 1.2 meters high, placed parallel to the back wall, and there are three rope hanging protrusions on each side. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been found inside, but {{ill|Celadon|en|Celadon|qid=Q1054559|lt=Celadon|1=Celadon}} fragments thought to be from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} were found in the back chamber, so it is possible that the tomb was robbed during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}. The structure and size of the burial chamber suggest that the tumulus was built in the late 6th century, or the late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The tumulus is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|qid=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}} {{ill|Yukuhashi Station|en|Yukuhashi Station|qid=Q5360403|lt=Yukuhashi Station|1=Yukuhashi Station}}. ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=4&id=38 Cultural Properties of Fukuoka]{{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.town.miyako.lg.jp/rekisiminnzoku/kankou/kohun/kohun_4.html Miyako Town home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/13193 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
